data.title: Virtual Mind Seeks Shareholder Backing for Major Share Issue and Warrant Deal
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited ( (HK:1520) ) has issued an update.

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting in Hong Kong on 13 August 2026 to seek shareholder approval for a new equity subscription and related warrant issuance to GEM Global Yield LLC SCS. The proposed deal involves the subscription of up to 8,000,000 new shares at HK$0.85 each and the creation of up to 31,000,000 warrants exercisable at HK$1.97 per share, with directors to receive a specific mandate to issue the subscription and warrant shares once listing approval is granted.

The company is also asking shareholders to approve a conditional placing agreement with Wanhai Securities (HK) Limited for a best-effort placing of up to 100,000,000 shares at HK$0.52 per share. If approved, the transactions will significantly expand the company’s issued share capital, providing additional funding flexibility while diluting existing shareholders and potentially reshaping its capital structure and market positioning.

More about Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 1520. The company operates in Hong Kong and raises equity capital through share subscriptions, warrant issues and share placings to support its corporate and financing activities.

data.title: ISP Holdings Subsidiary Wins Key Court Rulings in HK$98.5 Million Contract Dispute
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

ISP Holdings Limited ( (HK:2340) ) has provided an announcement.

ISP Holdings’ construction subsidiary ISP Construction (Engineering) Limited has secured a partial court win in a major contractual dispute with ATAL Engineering Limited over an outstanding claim of about HK$98.5 million. The High Court of Hong Kong ruled that ATAL repudiated the contract, confirmed ISPCE’s entitlement to an acceleration claim, dismissed ATAL’s counterclaim, but found ISPCE had not proven prolongation costs.

The judgment covers only the first phase of a bifurcated trial, with the second phase in August 2026 to decide the quantum of sums recoverable, leaving the financial impact on ISP Holdings still uncertain. The company has urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution as the eventual monetary award could materially affect its financial position and will provide further updates as required under Hong Kong listing rules.

More about ISP Holdings Limited

ISP Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries including ISP Construction (Engineering) Limited, which is engaged in construction-related engineering services. The group focuses on project-based contracts in Hong Kong, exposing it to complex contractual and legal disputes typical of the construction industry.

Average Trading Volume: 213,891

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$126.9M

data.title: COFCO Joycome warns of heavy first-half loss on weak hog prices
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

COFCO Joycome Foods Limited ( (HK:1610) ) has provided an update.

COFCO Joycome Foods Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the agri-food sector with a focus on hog production, branded meat products and feed manufacturing. The group emphasizes end-to-end value chain management, including breeding R&D, procurement, production and health management, while expanding its branded and feed businesses through product innovation and wider sales channels.

The company warned that it expects a loss of about RMB1.0 billion to RMB1.2 billion for the first half of 2026, compared with a profit of RMB198 million a year earlier. Management attributed the swing to lower hog selling prices squeezing margins, noting that cost reductions, hedging with hog futures and growth in branded and feed businesses were insufficient to offset the price pressure.

The announcement underscores the vulnerability of its hog production segment to market price cycles, even as operational efficiencies and diversification efforts are strengthened. Shareholders and potential investors have been cautioned ahead of the formal interim results release due in late August, highlighting heightened earnings risk for stakeholders in the near term.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1610) stock is a Buy
with a HK$1.80 price target.

see the HK:1610 Stock Forecast page.

More about COFCO Joycome Foods Limited

COFCO Joycome Foods Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the agri-food sector with a focus on hog production, branded meat products and feed manufacturing. The group emphasizes end-to-end value chain management, including breeding R&D, procurement, production and health management, while expanding its branded and feed businesses through product innovation and wider sales channels.

The company has been building its market presence by investing in new product development, brand building and channel expansion to drive growth in its branded meat operations. Its feed business remains oriented toward product innovation and market expansion, delivering year-on-year growth in business scale despite volatility in upstream hog prices.

Central Wealth Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:0139) ) has provided an announcement.

Central Wealth Group Holdings Limited has agreed to dispose of a significant block of listed CMBC shares through two conditional sale and purchase agreements with independent individual merchants, involving a total of 31,782,250 shares for aggregate consideration of HK$95.347 million. The transaction, executed after trading hours on 24 July 2026 via its wholly owned BVI subsidiary, qualifies as a major transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, requiring shareholder approval and reflecting an ongoing realignment of the group’s investment portfolio that could affect its exposure to CMBC and capital allocation strategy.

Under Agreement A, Purchaser A will acquire 19,782,250 CMBC shares for HK$59.347 million, largely prepaid in cash and treated as a deposit secured by a transfer of the shares, with mechanisms for refund and re-transfer should conditions precedent not be met. Agreement B sees Purchaser B buying 12,000,000 CMBC shares for HK$36 million, also treated as a deposit before completion, underscoring a structured, risk-managed disposal process designed to ensure flexibility if regulatory or contractual conditions are not satisfied, while providing immediate liquidity to the company.

More about Central Wealth Group Holdings Limited

data.title: Ausnutria Dairy Flags H1 Loss on One-Off Charges but Stresses Stable Core Business
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ausnutria Dairy Corp. Ltd. ( (HK:1717) ) has shared an update.

Ausnutria Dairy warned that revenue for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB3.07 billion–3.17 billion from RMB3.89 billion a year earlier, with the group swinging to a net loss of RMB685 million–785 million versus a prior net profit. The company cited industry changes, international logistics disruptions, tighter regulations and cost pressures, as well as one-off inventory adjustments and non-cash asset impairments, although it expects core net operating profit of RMB155 million–255 million and says underlying fundamentals remain stable.

Management said it is reshaping channel structures, tightening inventory management and streamlining its product portfolio and overseas operations to support long-term growth, even at the cost of temporary adjustment expenses. In response to recent share price volatility, the board signalled plans to use its existing share repurchase mandate when conditions allow, arguing buybacks could enhance per-share value and demonstrate confidence in the group’s long-term prospects.

More about Ausnutria Dairy Corp. Ltd.

Ausnutria Dairy Corporation Ltd. is a Hong Kong–listed dairy company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, focused on infant milk formula and related dairy products. The group operates primarily through production bases in Europe and manages an international supply chain, positioning it within the global infant nutrition market.

data.description:

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1536) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. plans a non-underwritten rights issue of up to 1.368 billion shares on the basis of three rights shares for every one share held, at HK$0.10 per rights share, to raise gross proceeds of about HK$136.8 million. Net proceeds of approximately HK$134.9 million are earmarked for settling deferred payments, paying the exercise price of a call option, funding business development at a target company, expanding operations into its investment property after current tenancies expire and bolstering group working capital.

The rights issue will only be available to qualifying shareholders, with no excess application mechanism and no minimum subscription level, and any unsubscribed shares will be placed on a best-effort basis under compensatory arrangements. To avoid triggering a mandatory takeover offer, shareholder applications for their assured entitlements may be scaled down, while any rights shares left unsold after the placing will not be issued, effectively reducing the final size of the capital raise.

More about Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd.

Yuk Wing Group Holdings Ltd. is a Hong Kong‑listed company focused on diversified investments, including interests in a target company that holds an investment property. The group’s operations rely on access to capital markets to fund deferred payments, option exercises and ongoing business development, alongside general working capital needs.

Angelalign Technology Inc. has granted 780,258 restricted share units to 255 employees under its post-IPO RSU scheme, with the awards representing an equivalent number of ordinary shares and including one member of senior management. The company states that none of the grantees are directors, chief executives, substantial shareholders, or related entity participants breaching individual or aggregate limits under Hong Kong listing rules, and that no purchase price is payable for the grants.

The RSUs will vest in four tranches of 30%, 30%, 20% and 20% between September 2026 and September 2029, with part of the vesting period falling under 12 months, which the board and remuneration committee deem appropriate to maintain competitive compensation. Vesting is tied to performance targets assessed through the group’s comprehensive evaluation system, suggesting the company is using equity incentives to align key employees’ interests with long-term operational performance and industry practices, potentially bolstering talent retention and execution of its strategic plans.

Wang On Group and China Agri-Products Exchange have entered into a sale and leaseback arrangement through their subsidiaries Qinzhou Hongjin and Kaifeng Hongjin, selling certain leased assets to Haier for RMB64 million, equivalent to about HK$73.81 million. The transaction, agreed after arm’s length negotiations and supported by equity pledge registrations from related entities, provides liquidity while allowing continued use of the assets, and qualifies as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong Listing Rules for both companies.

Under the terms, Haier will pay the sale price within 10 business days after receiving required receipts, payment notices, and confirmation of equity pledge registrations, reflecting a loan-to-value structure acceptable to the lessor. This financing move strengthens the groups’ capital flexibility without immediate operational disruption, potentially improving balance sheet management and signaling a strategic use of asset-backed funding to support ongoing projects and stakeholder confidence.

data.title: Shaanxi Micot Completes Phase II Data Lock for Stroke Drug MT200605
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2335) ) has issued an update.

Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. has completed the database lock for a Phase II clinical trial of its independently developed neuroprotective drug candidate MT200605 for acute ischemic stroke in China. MT200605 is an injectable agent that combines TrkB receptor activation with oxygen free radical scavenging to limit neuronal damage and promote regeneration in stroke patients.

The Phase II trial was a multi-center, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study evaluating the efficacy, safety and pharmacokinetics of MT200605 in 360 patients, with first enrollment in September 2025 and last patient out in June 2026. Completion of the database lock marks a key milestone in the program’s clinical development, paving the way for data analysis that could shape the company’s stroke portfolio and influence its competitive position in neurovascular therapeutics.

More about Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shaanxi Micot Pharmaceutical Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based pharmaceutical company focused on developing innovative injectable neuroprotective therapies. The group targets serious neurological conditions such as acute ischemic stroke, leveraging proprietary drug candidates designed to modulate neuronal survival, reduce oxidative damage and enhance neural repair in hospital and specialist care settings.

Lens Technology Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6613) ) just unveiled an update.

Lens Technology’s wholly owned subsidiary Lens International (HK) Limited has signed a memorandum of understanding with Intel Corporation to explore cooperation in through-glass via advanced packaging technology. The discussions will cover processes such as glass substrate via formation, high-precision laser processing and metal-filled via deposition, with any concrete engineering validation or volume production to be governed by separate agreements.

Beyond TGV, the parties plan to evaluate potential collaboration in structural components and modules for AI PCs, high-reliability thermal solutions for data center servers and hardware for embodied AI robotics and edge computing. Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2521) ) has shared an update.

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited announced a significant change in its shareholder structure after Prosperity Cleanness Investment Holdings Limited sold 175,476,150 shares, equal to about 9% of the company’s issued share capital. Following this disposal, Prosperity Cleanness’ stake fell from roughly 30.1% to 21.1%, meaning it no longer qualifies as a controlling shareholder under Hong Kong listing rules.

Prosperity Cleanness is wholly owned by co-chairman and executive director Li Chenghua, so the transaction reduces his effective control over the company while leaving him in a key management position. The company advised shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in its shares, signaling that the shift in ownership could affect perceptions of control, governance dynamics and future strategic decisions.

More about Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited

Shenghui Cleanness Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating in the cleaning and hygiene services or products sector. The group is led by a board comprising four executive directors, including co-chairman and CEO Li Chenghua, and three independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance structure for a mid-sized listed firm in the region.

Average Trading Volume: 3,247,375

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$2.11B

data.title: China Power flags up to 57% profit drop on weaker wind and solar, softer coal demand
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) has issued an announcement.

China Power International Development warned that profit attributable to ordinary shareholders for the first half of 2026 is expected to fall to about RMB1.1 billion to RMB1.4 billion, a drop of roughly 45% to 57% from a year earlier. The company cited weaker wind and solar resources, heightened tariff volatility in China’s competitive power market and lower coal-fired electricity sales as key drivers of the earnings decline.

Management said strong rainfall boosted hydropower output at subsidiary SPIC Hydropower, delivering a significant profit increase that partially offset the shortfall in other segments. The figures are based on unaudited internal accounts, and the company plans to release its formal interim results in late August 2026, while advising shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its shares.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold
with a HK$3.30 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock,
see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong–incorporated power producer focused on generating electricity from a diversified mix of wind, solar, hydropower and coal-fired plants across mainland China. The group, listed in Hong Kong, also controls Shanghai-listed SPIC Hydropower, highlighting its strategic emphasis on renewable and low-carbon energy assets within China’s evolving power market.
